---
title: "Phase 3 — truth-catalog generation"
subtitle: "Matched stars + galaxies, Sobol-distributed, per selected skycell"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pathlib import Path
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from astropy.table import Table

REPO = Path("../..").resolve()
SKYCELLS = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/selected_skycells.ecsv",
                      format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
INDEX = Table.read(REPO / "catalogs/detection/catalogs_index.ecsv",
                   format="ascii.ecsv").to_pandas()
```

## What Phase 3 does

For each skycell selected in [Phase 2](phase2_skycells.qmd), generate
**two truth catalogs** — one of point sources, one of galaxies — at
**disjoint** positions across the skycell. Stars and galaxies sit at
different RA/Dec so that detection sees them as separate sources; the
two catalogs share an identical magnitude distribution, so their
efficiency curves can be overlaid directly.

Each catalog holds **210 sources = 21 magnitude bins × 10 sources per
bin**, magnitudes running 23.0–26.0 in 0.15-mag steps. Fluxes are stored
as maggies in the `F158` column (`= 10^(-mag/2.5)`) — the unit romanisim
expects.

The galaxy catalog sets `type="SER"` with Sersic `n=1`,
`half_light_radius = 0.275"`, `pa=0`, `ba=1` (circular exponential).
The stars catalog sets `type="PSF"` (PSF-only).

## Placement — Sobol quasi-random

Instead of a regular grid (which would correlate magnitude with pixel
column and could interact with SCA boundaries or PSF variations), we
use a **2-D Sobol low-discrepancy sequence** inside the skycell's
unique core. The magnitude assignment is a separate seeded shuffle,
so every mag bin's 10 sources are scattered across the full skycell
area — no systematic mag-vs-position correlation.

Skycells overlap their neighbours by `skycell_border_pixels = 100`
(per the Roman skymap reference file), so the unique core of each
5000×5000 skycell is `[100, 4900]²`. We inject sources inside
`[200, 4800]²` — a 200-pixel margin that puts every source 100 pixels
inside the unique core, with room to spare for PSF wings and drizzle
kernels.

420 Sobol points are generated per skycell; the first 210 become the
stars catalog, the second 210 the galaxies catalog. With the full
420 spread over a 4600×4600 pix injection area at 0.055″/pix, mean
nearest-neighbour separation is ~6″, with a small tail down to ~3″ —
well above the PSF FWHM (~0.15″) and the galaxy half-light radius
(0.275″), so detection sees all sources individually.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "Pair 11119 skycell 000m18x60y42 source layout. LEFT: 210 stars (circles) and 210 galaxies (squares) at disjoint Sobol positions, coloured by F158 magnitude; dashed rectangle = the 200-pixel injection boundary, dotted = the 100-px skycell-overlap border. RIGHT: nearest-neighbour separation histogram across all 420 sources, with the nominal 5″ blending floor marked — well separated for both PSF and Sersic sources."
from IPython.display import Image, display
display(Image(filename=str(
    REPO / "output/detection/phase3/grid_000m18x60y42.png")))
```

## Catalog index

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "One row per skycell catalog pair. Each Phase-4 L2 sim reads the combined stars+galaxies file for its pair's skycell; the per-source `type` column tells romanisim which is which."
INDEX[["PAIR_ID", "skycell_name", "skycell_idx",
       "n_stars", "n_galaxies", "mag_min", "mag_max",
       "stars_path", "galaxies_path"]]
```

## Schema

The on-disk parquet format matches the existing `catalogs/sources.parquet`
column convention (see `docs/data_model_reference.md`) so romanisim
can ingest it without any adapter:

```{python}
#| echo: false
import pyarrow.parquet as pq
# Show the first row of the 11119 stars catalog
path = REPO / INDEX.iloc[-1]["stars_path"]
t = pq.read_table(path)
schema_rows = [(f.name, str(f.type)) for f in t.schema]
pd.DataFrame(schema_rows, columns=["column", "dtype"])
```

A sample row from the 11119 **stars** catalog (`type = PSF`):

```{python}
#| echo: false
import numpy as np
rng = np.random.default_rng(17)
k = int(rng.integers(0, t.num_rows))
t.slice(k, 1).to_pandas().T.rename(columns={0: "value"})
```

A sample row from the 11119 **galaxies** catalog
(`type = SER`, `n = 1`, `half_light_radius = 0.275″`) — different
position, same magnitude distribution:

```{python}
#| echo: false
gpath = REPO / INDEX.iloc[-1]["galaxies_path"]
gt = pq.read_table(gpath)
gt.slice(k, 1).to_pandas().T.rename(columns={0: "value"})
```

## Self-checks

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "End-of-phase assertions."
stars_11119 = Table.read(
    REPO / "catalogs/detection/catalogs/skycell_000m18x60y42_stars.parquet"
).to_pandas()
gal_11119 = Table.read(
    REPO / "catalogs/detection/catalogs/skycell_000m18x60y42_galaxies.parquet"
).to_pandas()

checks = [
    ("5 catalog pairs written (1 per selected skycell)",  len(INDEX) == 5),
    ("210 sources in every catalog",
     bool((INDEX["n_stars"] == 210).all() and
          (INDEX["n_galaxies"] == 210).all())),
    ("21 distinct magnitudes × 10 each = 210 (per type)",
     len(stars_11119["mag"].unique()) == 21 and
     bool((stars_11119["mag"].value_counts() == 10).all())),
    ("mag range [23.0, 26.0]",
     (np.isclose(stars_11119["mag"].min(), 23.0)
      and np.isclose(stars_11119["mag"].max(), 26.0))),
    ("stars and galaxies have disjoint positions",
     bool(len(set(zip(stars_11119["ra"].round(7), stars_11119["dec"].round(7)))
               & set(zip(gal_11119["ra"].round(7), gal_11119["dec"].round(7)))) == 0)),
    ("stars and galaxies share identical magnitude set",
     bool(np.allclose(sorted(stars_11119["mag"]), sorted(gal_11119["mag"])))),
    ("F158 = 10^(-mag/2.5)",
     bool(np.allclose(stars_11119["F158"].values,
                      10.0 ** (-stars_11119["mag"].values / 2.5)))),
    ("stars all type PSF",
     bool((stars_11119["type"] == "PSF").all())),
    ("galaxies all type SER with n=1, r=0.275",
     bool((gal_11119["type"] == "SER").all()
          and np.allclose(gal_11119["n"], 1.0)
          and np.allclose(gal_11119["half_light_radius"], 0.275))),
    ("pixel positions inside the 200-px injection boundary",
     bool((stars_11119["pix_x"].between(200, 4800)).all()
          and (stars_11119["pix_y"].between(200, 4800)).all()
          and (gal_11119["pix_x"].between(200, 4800)).all()
          and (gal_11119["pix_y"].between(200, 4800)).all())),
]
pd.DataFrame(checks, columns=["check", "pass"])
```

## Reproducing

```bash
pixi run python scripts/detection/03_generate_catalogs.py
# Or, for the single-skycell validation on pair 11119:
pixi run python scripts/detection/03_generate_catalogs.py --only-pair 11119
```

Writes per-skycell parquet files under
`catalogs/detection/catalogs/skycell_<name>_{stars,galaxies}.parquet`
plus a `catalogs_index.ecsv` summary and a `grid_<name>.png` QA plot
per skycell.

## Checkpoint

Phase 3 deliverable. Pair 11119 is the validation case — next step is
Phase 4a: generate its 14 L2 files, coadd to a single L3 mosaic, and
verify depth/coverage/image quality before scaling.
